content.problemStatement:
You live on a street with NNN houses. The houses are labelled as 111 222 . . . NNN, from left to right. You live in the house labelled 111, i.e the left-most house. and you wish to reach the house labelled NNN i.e. the right-most house.

There is a tunnel present in each house. The tunnel present in the housel labelled iii can be used to reach any house labelled jjj, such that j‚àíi‚â§Aij - i \leq A_{i}j‚àíi‚â§Ai‚Äã.

Determine the minimum number of houses you can visit in order to reach the right-most house.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each testcase contains NNN integers : A1,A2...ANA_1, A_2 . . . A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã...AN‚Äã.

It is guaranteed that the sum of the values of NNN over all the testcases doesn't exceed 3‚àó1063*10^63‚àó106.



Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single integer equal to the minimum number of houses you can visit in order to reach the right-most house.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test-case 1:
You use the tunnel present in the house labelled 111 to reach the house labelled 333. Thus, you visited 222 houses in total, the house labelled 111 and the house labelled 333.
Test-case 2:
One of the ways to reach the house labelled NNN is to

Use the tunnel in house labelled 111 to reach house labelled 222.
Use the tunnel in house labelled 222 to reach house labelled 666.
Use the tunnel in house labelled 666 to reach house labelled 888.

Thus we visit 444 houses in total. There exists no way to reach house labelled NNN by visiting less than 444 houses.
